attributes.description: Alerts when containers are using more than 90% of their memory limit. Containers approaching their memory limit will be OOMKilled, causing restarts and service disruption.
attributes.name: [K8s OTel] Container memory near limit

investigation_guide.blob:
## Container Memory Near Limit

### Triage Steps
1. Identify the affected container(s) from the alert results.
2. Open the Pod & Container Detail dashboard to see memory usage vs limits over time.
3. Determine if memory is steadily climbing (leak) or spiking (load-related).
4. Check current memory usage: `kubectl top pods -n <namespace> --containers`.
5. Review the container's memory limit — is it set appropriately for the workload?

### Common Causes
- Memory limit too low for actual workload requirements
- Memory leak in the application
- Increased traffic causing higher memory consumption
- In-memory caching growing without eviction
- JVM heap sized too close to the container memory limit

### Tuning
- The default threshold is 90% of the memory limit.
- Lower to 80% for early warning; raise to 95% to reduce noise.
- Exclude known memory-intensive containers that run close to their limits by design.

### Escalation
Containers above 90% memory utilisation are at imminent risk of OOMKill. Increase memory limits as a short-term mitigation and investigate the root cause.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-kubeletstatsreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE k8s.container.memory_limit_utilization IS NOT NULL
| STATS
    avg_util = AVG(k8s.container.memory_limit_utilization)
    BY k8s.pod.name, k8s.container.name, k8s.namespace.name, k8s.node.name
// Alert when average memory usage exceeds 90% of the container's limit.
// Adjust the 0.9 threshold to tune sensitivity:
//   0.80 = early warning (more alerts)
//   0.95 = imminent OOMKill risk (fewer alerts)
| WHERE avg_util > 0.9
| EVAL util_pct = ROUND(avg_util * 100, 1)
| SORT util_pct DESC
| KEEP k8s.namespace.name, k8s.pod.name, k8s.container.name, k8s.node.name, util_pct
| LIMIT 50
